cards.title: Amazon Tests Alexa+ in India with Hindi Support
cards.body: Amazon is expanding its Alexa+ conversational AI assistant to India with a Hindi-language version. The company is inviting Indian users to test the new localized AI assistant.

cards.title: Reflection AI Signs $150M/Month Compute Deal with SpaceX
cards.body: Reflection AI will pay $150 million monthly for access to Nvidia's GB300 AI chips through SpaceX's Colossus 2 data center near Memphis. The deal runs from July 2026 through 2029.

cards.title: Nobel Laureate John Jumper Leaves DeepMind for Anthropic
cards.body: DeepMind researcher and Nobel laureate John Jumper is joining rival AI company Anthropic. This marks another significant departure from Google's AI research division.

cards.title: US Bans Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Models
cards.body: The US government forced Anthropic to pull its two newest models citing national security concerns. The ban followed reports that Amazon researchers found a way to bypass Fable 5's safety guardrails.

cards.title: Elastic Acquires Deductive AI for Up to $85M
cards.body: Elastic is buying bug-detection AI startup Deductive AI for up to $85 million. The three-year-old company uses AI to automatically catch and resolve software bugs.

cards.title: Baseten Raises $1.5B at $13B Valuation
cards.body: AI inference startup Baseten is reportedly closing a $1.5 billion funding round months after its previous mega-round. The raise reflects the ongoing "inference gold rush" in AI infrastructure.

cards.title: Snap Spins Off AI Video Team as Dotmo
cards.body: Snapchat's parent company is spinning off its AI video team into a new independent company called Dotmo. The move comes as Snap seeks to control costs while advancing AI video development.

cards.title: OpenAI Hires Transformer Co-Inventor Before IPO
cards.body: OpenAI is bulking up ahead of its IPO, landing Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball. Both hires signal OpenAI's expansion in research and policy.

cards.title: Amazon Challenges Nvidia by Selling AI Chips
cards.body: AWS is in talks to sell its proprietary AI chips to other data centers. CEO Andy Jassy estimates this represents a $50 billion market opportunity for the company.

cards.title: Reliance Weaves AI Into Services for 500M Users
cards.body: Billionaire Ambani's Reliance is integrating AI across telecom services used by over 500 million people. The push aims to bring AI capabilities into calls, apps, and home devices.

cards.title: Apple Launches Practical AI Features in iOS 27
cards.body: Beyond Siri's AI overhaul, Apple is bringing several AI-powered features across iOS 27. These practical tools will be distributed throughout the operating system's various apps and functions.

cards.title: Signal's Whittaker: AI Chatbots Aren't Your Friends
cards.body: Signal executive Meredith Whittaker warns users that AI chatbots are not conscious beings or sentient. She emphasizes the importance of remembering what these systems actually are.

cards.title: Amazon Tests Alexa+ with Hindi Support in India
cards.body: Amazon is expanding its new conversational AI assistant Alexa+ to India with a Hindi-language version. The company is inviting Indian users to test the multilingual AI experience.

cards.title: Reflection AI Inks Massive Compute Deal with SpaceX
cards.body: Reflection AI will pay $150 million monthly through 2029 for access to Nvidia's latest GB300 AI chips via SpaceX's Colossus 2 data center. The deal grants immediate access to cutting-edge AI infrastructure.

cards.title: Trump Admin Cracks Down on Anthropic AI
cards.body: The administration has made new moves against AI company Anthropic, prompting discussion about implications for the broader AI ecosystem. The episode explores what prompted these latest actions.

cards.title: iOS 27 Brings Practical AI Features Beyond Siri
cards.body: Apple's iOS 27 includes multiple AI-powered features throughout the operating system, not just Siri improvements. The rollout brings practical machine learning capabilities to users' daily iPhone interactions.

cards.title: Signal's Meredith Whittaker Warns AI Chatbots Aren't Friends
cards.body: Signal's leader emphasizes that AI chatbots are not sentient beings or conscious interlocutors. The warning highlights the importance of realistic expectations around AI assistants.

cards.title: Nobel Laureate John Jumper Leaves DeepMind for Anthropic
cards.body: Jumper, a prestigious AI researcher and Nobel laureate, is departing Google DeepMind for rival AI company Anthropic. He joins other notable departures from the AI research division.

cards.title: US Bans Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Models
cards.body: The US government forced Anthropic to pull its two newest AI models citing national security concerns. The ban followed reports that Amazon researchers found ways to bypass Fable 5's safety guardrails.

cards.title: Reliance Weaves AI Into Telecom for 500M+ Users
cards.body: Billionaire Ambani's Reliance is integrating AI into telecom services reaching over 500 million people. The initiative aims to embed AI across calls, apps, and smart homes.

cards.title: Elastic Acquires Bug-Catching AI Startup Deductive
cards.body: Elastic has agreed to buy Deductive AI for up to $85 million, a startup using AI to identify and resolve software bugs. The three-year-old company represents the growing market for AI development tools.

cards.title: AI Inference Startup Baseten Raises $1.5 Billion
cards.body: Baseten is reportedly finalizing a $1.5 billion funding round at a $13 billion valuation. The raise reflects the ongoing "inference gold rush" in the AI market.

cards.title: Snap Spins Off Dotmo, AI Video Company
cards.body: Snapchat maker Snap is spinning off its AI video team into a new standalone company called Dotmo. The move reflects rising costs associated with AI development.

cards.title: OpenAI Hires Transformer Co-Inventor Before IPO
cards.body: OpenAI has brought on Noam Shazeer, a Transformer co-inventor from Google DeepMind, as it prepares for its IPO. The company also hired former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball.

cards.title: Nearly Half of US Singles Reject AI in Dating
cards.body: About 47% of US singles view AI negatively in dating apps, according to Match data. Many users remain open to AI helping with profile improvements and conversation starters.

cards.title: Amazon Challenges Nvidia by Selling AI Chips
cards.body: AWS is negotiating to sell its proprietary AI chips to other data centers, CEO Andy Jassy announced. The move represents a potential $50 billion opportunity for the company.

cards.title: Subquadratic Claims LLM Bottleneck Breakthrough
cards.body: Miami-based AI startup Subquadratic emerged from stealth claiming to have solved a mathematical bottleneck limiting large language models for nearly a decade. Details remain sparse, with skepticism in the research community.

cards.title: Brain-Computer Interface Trials Accelerate Rapidly
cards.body: Casey Harrell, paralyzed with ALS, has become the first major power user of brain implants, unable to speak without the device. He has successfully used the brain-computer interface for nearly three years.

cards.title: How Anthropic Talked Itself Into Export Ban
cards.body: Anthropic may have inadvertently contributed to US government restrictions on exporting its AI models. The ban raises questions about corporate transparency and regulatory consequences.
